items.title: B.C. premier says OpenAI CEO Sam Altman will apologize to Tumbler Ridge, push for stronger regulations
items.summary_auto: The company behind ChatGPT is facing waves of criticism after it came out that the account of Jesse Van Rootselaar — who police say killed eight people, including six children, in Tumbler Ridge on Feb. 10 — wasn't reported to police despite posts about gun violence.

items.title: The unanswered question in the U.S.-Israel war on Iran: Why? | About That
items.summary_auto: Questions are being raised about why the U.S. and Israel chose this particular moment to launch an attack on Iran. Andrew Chang lays out the mixed messaging from U.S. President Donald Trump and his administration to break down possible motivations behind the move and the criticism it faces — even among MAGA Republicans. (Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters and Getty Images)

items.title: HBO's future on Crave uncertain as Paramount Skydance acquires Warner Bros. Discovery in merger
items.summary_auto: It's not clear if the U.S. studio mega-merger will affect existing agreements that Warner Bros. Discovery has with Canadian broadcasters and streamers, like Crave, and whether Canadian viewers will have to subscribe to yet another service to watch their favourite shows.

items.title: Oil prices spike to highest level since summer of 2024 amid war in Iran
items.summary_auto: Stocks on Wall Street sank on Thursday after the price of oil spiked to its highest level since the summer of 2024 due to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.

items.title: Kristi Noem out at U.S. Department of Homeland Security
Jury finds man guilty of 5 counts of 2nd-degree murder in Winnipeg drug house shootings
items.summary_auto: A jury has found a man accused of fatally shooting five people at a Winnipeg rooming house in 2023 guilty of five counts of second-degree murder.

items.title: Rental company charged in fatal 2024 tent collapse at Alberta meditation retreat
items.summary_auto: Nearly two years after a tent collapse at a meditation retreat northwest of Edmonton left one woman dead and dozens of others injured, a special event rental company is facing charges. Investigators allege the structure was not safely installed, leaving it vulnerable to severe winds.

items.title: Canada's Alison Mackie skis to U23 cross-country gold, teammate Xavier McKeever earns bronze
items.summary_auto: Alison Mackie won gold in the women's 20-kilometre mass start and fellow Canadian Xavier McKeever took bronze in the men's event Thursday at the under-23 cross-country skiing championships in Lillehammer, Norway.

items.title: Canada signing agreement with EU to enhance free trade deal
items.summary_auto: As relations with the Trump administration remain fraught and uncertain, trade ministers from Canada and the European Union are set to sign on to a series of improvements to their bilateral trade agreement.

items.title: Montreal police arrest Canada's most wanted fugitive
items.summary_auto: Montreal police arrested Bryan Fuentes Gramajo, 24, overnight Wednesday. He was wanted in connection with the shooting death of 28-year-old Kashif Jamal Bentley-Jean last July in the parking lot of the Yorkdale Shopping Centre in Toronto.

items.title: Man charged with threatening Ontario Premier Doug Ford: OPP
items.summary_auto: A 20-year-old man has been charged with threatening Ontario Premier Doug Ford, provincial police said Thursday

items.title: Foreign Affairs Minister Anand says NATO must turn its focus to the North
items.summary_auto: Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand touted Canada's new commitment to defence spending and the domestic defence sector and called for a NATO policy pivot to the Arctic, at a major defence and security conference in Ottawa on Wednesday.

items.title: Canada not looking to split submarine contract between Germans, Koreans: secretary of state
items.summary_auto: Canada's secretary of state for defence procurement says the government is planning to choose just one company to build the country's next fleet of submarines.

items.title: What can Canada learn from Mexico about Chinese EVs?
items.summary_auto: Beginning this week, Chinese electric vehicle makers can apply to import EVs to Canada at a reduced tariff rate. CBC Radio's The Current was in Mexico City recently to test drive a Chinese EV. The vehicles have been available in Mexico for years.

items.title: Sarah J. Maas reveals 2 new books in romantasy series, admits it was 'a long time coming'
items.summary_auto: After five long years of waiting, bestselling author Sarah J. Maas has announced the release dates for not one but two new books in her wildly popular romantasy series A Court of Thorns and Roses.

items.title: Ukraine support remains front and centre amid new global crisis, says NATO chief
items.summary_auto: NATO Secretary General ​Mark Rutte said alliance members agreed ​they would find ways to continue supporting Ukraine in its war against Russia, even as the burgeoning conflict in the Middle East has some nations in the region seeking European help.

items.title: Britney Spears arrested in California on suspicion of driving under the influence
items.summary_auto: The singer was briefly held in detention before she was released on Thursday morning with a May 4 court date, according to local authorities.

items.title: Delici Dubai Style chocolate mousse recalled, Costco Canada says
items.summary_auto: Costco Wholesale is alerting customers of a recall of certain lot codes of the Delici Dubai Style chocolate mousse due to the possible presence of cashew nuts and macademia nuts that aren't declared on the label.

items.title: Ex-provincial prosecutor rips former colleagues’ handling of his own extortion case
items.summary_auto: Former provincial Crown prosecutor Tim O'Brien says he was kept in the dark and treated with a lack of respect by his former colleagues in a case where he was the victim of extortion.

items.title: Brady Bunch house in L.A. granted historic landmark status
items.summary_auto: L.A. City Council voted unanimously on Wednesday to designate the house in the San Fernando Valley a historic-cultural monument, which protects the property against demolition or major renovations.

items.title: Alberta road construction company charged with safety breaches after worker crushed
items.summary_auto: An Alberta road construction company is facing workplace safety charges nearly two years after one of their workers sustained life-altering crush injuries on the job.

items.title: Alberta promises billions for health care, but new hospital towers still years away
items.summary_auto: As the Alberta government promises to address pressures on hospitals and improve surgical wait times, while also grappling with a deficit budget, there are questions about where it is choosing to spend its money.

items.title: Federal research body in talks with University of Alberta to ensure EDI requirement compliance
items.summary_auto: Federal government lawyers have contacted the University of Alberta to make sure it's meeting equity requirements for the country’s most prestigious professorships. It comes after the university proposed to remove equity, diversity and inclusion (EDI) from its hiring policy.

items.title: How to watch the Milano-Cortina Paralympics on CBC
'We feel betrayed': Disability community says N.S. budget cuts to day programs, caregiver benefit are 'illogical'
items.summary_auto: Parents and advocates are sounding the alarm over the harm the provincial government’s proposed budget cuts will cause some of Nova Scotia’s most vulnerable.

items.title: Seniors at independent living facility outraged after housing authority ends in-house care
items.summary_auto: The Nova Scotia Provincial Housing Agency is changing how continuing care is delivered at facilities in Bridgetown, Berwick and Antigonish due to operational changes and complaints by some residents about mandatory costs, according to a spokesperson.

items.title: 'We feel betrayed': Disability community says provincial budget 'punches down'
items.summary_auto: Parents and advocates are sounding the alarm over the harm the provincial government’s proposed budget cuts will cause some of Nova Scotia’s most vulnerable.

items.title: Indigenous ancestral remains found on Ontario property could cost this couple $319K
items.summary_auto: An Ontario couple was concerned to discover Indigenous ancestral remains on their property during renovations. But the biggest shock came when they learned a provincially mandated investigation could cost $319,000. Experts say the remains must be protected and treated with dignity — but that the current law should be updated so situations like this don't happen.

items.title: 'Scientifically not a good idea,' says researcher whose work informed B.C.'s daylight-time decision
items.summary_auto: Scientists who study sleep are warning B.C. 's decision to adopt permanent daylight time will disrupt sleep patterns and have a negative effect on overall health, especially for children, for whom adequate sleep is critical for brain development.

items.title: Canadians reaching out to Spain after Trump's 'sadly familiar' threats
items.summary_auto: Some Canadians are feeling kinship with Spain and reaching out on social media after the European country was the latest victim of a tirade from U.S. President Donald Trump. "We're going to cut off all trade with Spain, we don't want anything to do with Spain," Trump told reporters at the White House on Tuesday.

items.title: 'Red flag' gun laws under scrutiny after Tumbler Ridge, B.C., shooting
items.summary_auto: Red flag orders were a key part of the federal government's response to the 2020 Portapique, N.S., mass shooting. But two years after the measure became law, a CBC News investigation has determined that no one is keeping track of when, where or how often these emergency orders are being implemented.

items.title: She was recruited by Quebec. Now she may not be able to stay under new immigration rules
items.summary_auto: Social worker Florence Bollet Michel was recruited abroad to work in Quebec. After jumping over the pond with her family and putting in two years of work in Montreal, her future in the province is now uncertain due to changes the government made to its immigration pathway programs.

items.title: How America’s war with Iran has shattered the U.A.E.’s sense of security
items.summary_auto: For decades, the United Arab Emirates has positioned itself as an economic and cultural hub, connecting European and Asian markets. But on Saturday, just hours after the U.S. and Israel launched massive strikes against Iran that killed the country’s longtime Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the U.A.E. came under fire like never before.

items.title: Airlines weigh risks in Mideast skies filled with 'hot pieces of flying metal'
items.summary_auto: Confusing advice and on-again-off-again airport closures are complicating the evacuation of hundreds of thousands of foreigners stranded in the conflict zone around the Persian Gulf. In a region that has seen tragic mistakes resulting in the deaths of hundreds of civilian air passengers, decisions on whether to fly remain fairly ad hoc and can come down to the judgment of an individual pilot.
